_comment: Curated model-provider registry for Urfael. The brain is the `claude` CLI, which any Anthropic-Messages-API endpoint can back via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL — so 'first-class multi-model' means managing those endpoints like connectors, not hosting weights. The flat-rate Claude SUBSCRIPTION is the default (id=claude) and the thesis; every non-Anthropic row runs on YOUR own key or YOUR own hardware. `urfael model use <id>` switches provider; `urfael model opus|sonnet` still pins the tier WITHIN the active provider. Point URFAEL_PROVIDERS_INDEX at your own index.json (same schema).
_schema: id, label, kind(anthropic|openai|gemini|ollama|bedrock|vertex), baseUrl(https|loopback|empty=native cloud), big_model, small_model, models[{id,role,ctx,tools}], authEnv(env var NAME only), authLabel, authKind(none|key|local|awschain|oauth), localToken, proxy(none|router|litellm), proxyHint, note, verified
_honesty: A local 7B-70B model is NOT Claude: weaker reasoning/code, shorter effective context, slower on consumer GPUs. Proxy/registry REACH is not hosting; Urfael runs no inference and brokers no aggregator's model count. A proprietary model with no public Anthropic-format endpoint is deliberately absent, never padded in. verified:false = the endpoint/models weren't pinned at research time; the preview shows the exact target before any write.

providers.label: Claude (flat-rate subscription)
providers.note: The default. Your Claude Code subscription, native cloud, $0 marginal. Tier pin uses Claude Code model aliases.

providers.label: Claude on AWS Bedrock
providers.note: Claude models billed to your AWS account; uses the AWS credential chain / region.

providers.label: Claude on Google Vertex
providers.note: Claude models billed to your GCP project; uses GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS / region.

providers.label: OpenRouter (your key)
providers.proxyHint: needs a translating proxy (claude-code-router/LiteLLM) unless using OpenRouter's Anthropic-compatible endpoint; confirm the base URL.
providers.note: Hundreds of models on your OpenRouter bill. Translation can subtly differ from a native /v1/messages endpoint.

providers.label: DeepSeek (your key)
providers.proxyHint: via a translating proxy; confirm the base URL + model ids.
providers.note: Strong/cheap, on your DeepSeek bill. Not Claude-grade on agentic tool use.

providers.label: Ollama (local GPU)
providers.proxyHint: Ollama's /v1 is OpenAI-shaped; a translating proxy is usually needed. No prompt caching, no tool_choice.
providers.note: Nothing leaves the machine; $0; air-gapped. A local model is weaker/slower than Claude (see _honesty).

providers.label: LM Studio (local GPU)
providers.proxyHint: OpenAI-shaped local server; via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Fully local. Same capability tradeoff as any local model.

providers.label: vLLM (local/self-host)
providers.proxyHint: vLLM silently fails tool-calls with the wrong --tool-call-parser; set it correctly. Via a translating proxy.
providers.note: Self-hosted throughput server. Tool reliability depends on the parser config.

providers.label: claude-code-router (your config)
providers.proxyHint: start the router first (`ccr` / `ccr code`); it routes to whatever providers YOU configured in its own config.
providers.note: You run the router; Urfael just points at 127.0.0.1. Multi-provider via one local hop.

providers.label: LiteLLM proxy (your config)
providers.proxyHint: start LiteLLM first; PIN a clean version (avoid the 1.82.7/1.82.8 advisory). Routes to your configured providers.
providers.note: You run + secure the proxy; Urfael points at 127.0.0.1.
